commands.review_summary:
The PR binds LM Studio setup wizard prompter `text` and `note` methods before storing them as callbacks, and adds regression coverage for class-method prompters.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +85. Total +87 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and v2026.6.1 extract unbound callbacks while the gateway prompter uses class methods that call `this.prompt`; the linked Windows issue provides concrete steps and before-fix visual evidence.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] No automated repair lane is needed because there is no blocking code finding; the remaining action is contributor proof plus normal maintainer review.

target.title: Fix LM Studio wizard prompter binding
target.body:
## Summary
- Bind LM Studio setup prompter methods before storing them as callbacks so gateway wizard class methods keep their receiver.
- Add regression coverage for both `text` and `note` class-method prompter paths.

## Repro / behavior proof
- Refs openclaw/openclaw-windows-node#682.
- A-side: Windows setup wizard on gateway `2026.5.28`, selecting **More… → LM Studio**, reproduced `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'prompt')`.
- B-side: patched the installed gateway bundle with the same binding change, restarted `openclaw-gateway.service`, and the same headless LM Studio provider probe advanced to `LM Studio base URL` instead of the TypeError.
- Manual UI verification after the installed-gateway patch also reached the `LM Studio base URL` step.
- Provider scan: LM Studio was the only provider option that hit the immediate prompt TypeError; other providers advanced to their next wizard step.

## Verification
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm test extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.test.ts` — 38 passed
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.ts extensions/lmstudio/src/setup.test.ts`
- `pnpm lint:extensions`
- Hanselman dual-model review: no blocking findings. Both reviewers flagged missing `note` binding coverage; this PR now includes that regression test.

## Notes
- `pnpm check:changed` could not run locally because the Crabbox/Testbox wrapper failed its local binary sanity check before starting the remote check.
- Windows consumption should wait for a fixed gateway release/LKG decision; this PR only fixes the gateway-side provider bug.
target.author: christineyan4
